description_long: This is an irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly rough, matte surface and a pale, off-white coloration. One side features a distinct, fossil-like spiral pattern embedded in the stone, suggesting a sedimentary origin. The stone's surfaces vary in texture, with some faces appearing smoother and others more jagged and fractured. The coloration ranges from light beige to darker grayish-brown, especially on the broader, flatter face. The stone appears solid and dense, with no visible cavities or openings. Its size and form suggest it could serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: An irregular, pale stone with a rough surface and a visible fossil-like spiral on one side. The stone features both jagged and smoother faces, with color variations from off-white to grayish-brown.
description_view_0: This view highlights the fossil-like spiral pattern embedded in the stone and shows the rough, pale surface with some jagged edges.
description_view_1: The stone appears narrow from this angle, with the fossil edge visible and the rough, angular profile emphasized.
description_view_2: A broad, dark grayish-brown face dominates this view, with minimal visible texture details and no fossil pattern apparent.
description_view_3: This side shows a mostly flat, pale surface with subtle texture, and the fossil edge is just barely visible along the side.
attribute_location: urban park landscaping or garden path
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: A prominent spiral fossil pattern on one side, with irregular mottling and natural fractures across other surfaces.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered, with some smoother areas and visible signs of natural erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured urban elements but can blend with natural landscaping, potentially partially surrounded by soil or low vegetation.
